description: UX quality standards for every Sovereign-generated app — grounded in Don Norman and Steve Krug principles.

don_norman.affordances: Every interactive element must visually communicate how to interact with it (buttons look clickable, inputs look typeable)
don_norman.feedback: Every user action must produce immediate, clear feedback — no silent failures
don_norman.discoverability: Users must be able to discover all features without reading a manual
don_norman.constraints: Prevent errors before they happen — disable buttons that can't be used, validate inline
don_norman.mapping: Controls must be laid out to match their effect — related actions grouped together

steve_krug.dont_make_me_think: Users should not have to figure out how the UI works — obvious is better than clever
steve_krug.omit_needless_words: Every word in the UI must earn its place — remove anything that doesn't help users act
steve_krug.design_for_scanning: Users scan, not read — use headings, bullets, and visual hierarchy to support scanning
steve_krug.happy_path_focus: Design for the most common task first, then handle edge cases

loading.rule: Every async operation must show a loading state
loading.pattern: Spinner, skeleton, or 'Loading...' text — never a frozen button with no feedback
loading.sovereign_standard: Button becomes disabled + shows spinner + text changes to 'Loading...' during async calls

error.rule: Every async operation must have an error state with a recovery action
error.pattern: Error message that explains what went wrong + a button to retry or correct
error.sovereign_standard: Never show a raw error code or stack trace to users — translate to human language with a fix

empty.rule: Every list or data view must have an empty state
empty.pattern: Illustration or icon + headline + subtext + primary call to action
empty.sovereign_standard: Empty state should tell users what the data will look like when it exists, not just 'No data found'

success.pattern: Toast notification, success message, or state transition — never silent success
success.sovereign_standard: Success state must be distinct from the loading state — users should know when the action completed

error_message_templates.network_error: Something went wrong. Check your connection and try again.
error_message_templates.auth_error: Incorrect email or password. Please try again.
error_message_templates.rate_limit: Too many requests. Please wait a moment before trying again.
error_message_templates.not_found: We couldn't find what you were looking for.
error_message_templates.server_error: Something went wrong on our end. We've been notified. Please try again.
error_message_templates.rule: Error messages must: (1) explain the problem, (2) suggest the fix, (3) never blame the user

interaction_patterns.transitions: Key elements should fade+translateY on entrance (200ms ease-out) — not instant pop-in
interaction_patterns.hover_states: All interactive elements must have a hover state that communicates interactivity
interaction_patterns.focus_states: All interactive elements must have a visible focus ring (keyboard navigation)
interaction_patterns.disabled_states: Disabled elements should appear muted (opacity 0.5) and have a tooltip explaining why
interaction_patterns.destructive_actions: Actions that delete or remove data must require confirmation — never one-click delete

no_lorem_ipsum.rule: No placeholder text in any UI — not even in development
no_lorem_ipsum.rationale: Lorem ipsum makes the design look unfinished and makes it impossible to review copy quality
no_lorem_ipsum.sovereign_standard: Every UI element ships with real, final copy

mobile_responsiveness.rule: Every page must be fully functional at 375px mobile width
mobile_responsiveness.touch_targets: Minimum 44x44px touch target size for all interactive elements (WCAG)
mobile_responsiveness.testing: Test every new component at 375px before shipping

onboarding_principles: Show value before asking for commitment (email, credit card)
Progress indicators reduce anxiety during multi-step flows
Confirm understanding at the end of onboarding — 'Your app is live at...'
No more than 3 required fields in any registration form
Inline validation — show errors as users type, not on submit
references: Don Norman — 'The Design of Everyday Things'
Sovereign RULES.md — the Jony Ive bar for generated apps
